reviews.review_description: ...but kept resetting to the beginning whenever I tried to take a screenshot.

Feels more like a prologue setting up a story. A story has conflict, rising action and then a climax. This has none of this. I don't know why it's three chapters when it could have been one. 

There are some nicely drawn scenes but the low resolution spoils it. The voice and sound is ok but it's just narration. The VR element doesn't add much which is a real shame too. This could have been much better.

The whole experience lasts 7 minutes. It's a recipe for boredom: uninspiring artwork, subpar narration, zero interactivity. Even what should be simple (displaying 360 degrees images) doesn't work right: the projection or mesh produces deformed images (where straight lines appear bent on some places). I recommend Nanite Fulcrum and Annihilator VR as much better experiences.
